# plot_ortho { #neuroim.plot_ortho }

```python
plot_ortho(
    vol,
    coords=None,
    title=None,
    cmap='gray',
    figsize=(12, 4),
    axes=None,
)
```

Orthogonal 3-plane view (axial, sagittal, coronal).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name    | Type                                     | Description                                                                              | Default    |
|---------|------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| vol     | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | 3-D volume array.                                                                        | _required_ |
| coords  | tuple of int                             | ``(x, y, z)`` voxel coordinates for the crosshair.  If *None* the volume centre is used. | `None`     |
| title   | [str](`str`)                             | Super-title for the figure.                                                              | `None`     |
| cmap    | [str](`str`)                             | Colormap name.                                                                           | `'gray'`   |
| figsize | [tuple](`tuple`)                         | Figure size in inches.                                                                   | `(12, 4)`  |
| axes    | array-like of Axes                       | Three pre-existing axes to draw into.  When provided *figsize* is ignored.               | `None`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                | Description                                  |
|--------|---------------------|----------------------------------------------|
|        | (fig, axes) : tuple | Matplotlib Figure and ndarray of three Axes. |
